threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Tube' instead of just 'London transport' like we all do. It's like wearing a new badge no one else recognizes.
Last week on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old soul tracks. A few joined in, but the driver just rolled his eyes and kept driving.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full suit arguing with a street musician over a beat drop at the Rathaus Neukölln. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous techno parties in the middle of a park thanks to the Berlinale's after-parties, mixing art and chaos like nowhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission when they can name all the taco trucks and tell you which one has the best lengua that's open past midnight.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus packed with people on their phones, but the lady next to me was reading a physical book. Felt so out of place in 2023.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how late we eat; dinner at 7 PM is practically breakfast in Gràcia. Restaurant staff look at you like you’re lost.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their confused faces near the 'café con leche' area; they still ask for a 'latte' instead of embracing the local lingo.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to the Clapton Pond as a park, not realising it’s more like a glorified puddle where the locals go to watch ducks bicker.
Caught a glimpse of a guy serenading the night bus crew with his guitar at Brixton. Half the passengers joined in, a real community vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a mom on Karl-Marx-Straße bargaining with a guy selling churros while her kid danced to the street musician's guitar. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that serves beer in bath tubs. Sounds odd, but it's a whole vibe after a long day!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they’ve witnessed a full BART evacuation and just shrug it off as another Tuesday commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 5 taking a nap while holding a half-eaten burrito. Know you’re in the TL when lunch and rest are one and the same.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the local bars are all about tapas. But the real charm? The hidden vermuterias where the regulars sip on homemade vermut and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they try to pay with a card at a cash-only churro stand. That look of confusion is priceless.
